conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions. Let me start with a basic one:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 15 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I understand you have one child, Alex, who is 15 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions. Let me start with:

Is Alex currently living with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's helpful to know.

Next question: Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you look after him and pay for things like his food, clothes, and housing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for him - I look after him and pay for all his needs like food, clothes, and housing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. 

Now I need to ask about your residency status: Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside in the UK (for example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, or another qualifying immigration status)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for providing that information.

One more important question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** Based on the information you've provided, you are **eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:
- Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement
- You are responsible for Alex - you look after him and pay for his needs
- Alex lives with you
- You live in the UK and have the right to reside here
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

All the eligibility criteria are met, so you should be able to successfully claim Child Benefit for Alex. If you'd like to proceed with your claim, you can apply through the government's official channels.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 15 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant is responsible for Alex, meaning they look after him and pay for his needs including food, clothes, and housing. Alex lives with the claimant. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here. Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 15), who lives with them. The claimant is responsible for Alex's care and financial needs, lives in the UK with the right to reside, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility requirements are met.
